deep_findings.title: ensureDetailedLinesLoadedForRating duplicated across state machine and run service
deep_findings.description: The method `ensureDetailedLinesLoadedForRating` exists in two places with different signatures and ownership models: as a method on `*stateMachine` in `openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/service/statemachine.go` (mutates `s.Charge` in place, no return value) and as a method on `*Service` in `openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/service/run/create.go` (returns an updated charge). Both lazy-fetch detailed lines from the adapter when the charge's realizations are missing them. The state machine version calls `s.Adapter.FetchDetailedLines` directly, duplicating logic that should be owned by the run.Service version. This is a classic AI copy-paste pattern: the same lazy-load logic was replicated with a slightly different signature to fit a different receiver context.
deep_findings.pattern_reference: AGENTS.md: 'Keep domain test helpers under openmeter/.../testutils independent from app/common — build test dependencies from underlying package constructors'; the run service owns run mechanics and should be the single place for detailed-lines lazy-loading logic.
deep_findings.recommendation: Extract the lazy-load logic into a single function in the `run` package (or a shared helper visible to both). The `stateMachine` should delegate to `run.Service.EnsureDetailedLinesLoadedForRating` rather than calling the adapter directly.

deep_findings.title: FinalizeRealizationRun (creditsonly) and SnapshotInvoiceUsage (creditheninvoice) share near-identical bodies
deep_findings.description: In `openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/service/creditsonly.go`, `FinalizeRealizationRun` and in `openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/service/creditheninvoice.go`, `SnapshotInvoiceUsage` execute structurally identical sequences: (1) nil-check `CurrentRealizationRunID`, (2) call `ensureDetailedLinesLoadedForRating`, (3) `GetByID` to fetch latest state, (4) compute storedAtOffset, (5) call rater for `GetDetailedLinesForUsage`, (6) round totals with `CurrencyCalculator`, (7) call `run.ReconcileCredits`, (8) call `run.PersistRunDetailedLines`, (9) call `run.UpdateRealizationRun`, (10) clear run state, (11) `UpdateCharge`, (12) `RefetchCharge`. The only behavioral differences are the `storedAtOffset` computation (credits-only uses `0`, credit-then-invoice uses a non-zero offset) and the `CreditAllocationMode` flag (`CreditAllocationExact` vs `CreditAllocationAvailable`). This is a textbook case of the semantic duplication pitfall documented in the project: two methods in parallel state machines that should share a common `realizeRun(ctx, opts realizationOpts)` private helper.
deep_findings.pattern_reference: AGENTS.md pitfalls section: AI copy-paste pattern. run/CLAUDE.md: 'Service owns run mechanics but not state-machine decisions' — the realization sequence is a run mechanic and should not be duplicated across two state machine files.
deep_findings.recommendation: Extract the shared realization sequence into a private helper on the base `stateMachine` struct (e.g., `func (s *stateMachine) executeRealizationRun(ctx, opts realizationRunOpts) error`) parameterized by storedAtOffset and CreditAllocationMode. Both `FinalizeRealizationRun` and `SnapshotInvoiceUsage` become thin wrappers that supply the correct opts and delegate.

deep_findings.title: upsertDetailedLines/upsertDetailedLinesV2 and upsertDetailedLineAmountDiscounts/upsertDetailedLineAmountDiscountsV2 are near-identical function pairs
deep_findings.description: In `openmeter/billing/adapter/stdinvoicelines.go`, `upsertDetailedLines` and `upsertDetailedLinesV2` have near-identical bodies: both iterate over input detailed lines, build an Ent create/update chain, handle ON CONFLICT, and return mapped results. The only difference is the target Ent builder type (`*db.BillingInvoiceLineCreate` for v1 vs `*db.BillingStandardInvoiceDetailedLineCreate` for v2). The same duplication exists between `upsertDetailedLineAmountDiscounts` and `upsertDetailedLineAmountDiscountsV2`. The SchemaLevel branching in `UpsertInvoiceLines` calls one pair or the other, but the logic bodies are structurally identical. This violates the AGENTS.md principle against semantic duplication and inflates the maintenance surface: a bug fix or field addition must be applied to both versions.
deep_findings.pattern_reference: AGENTS.md pitfalls: semantic duplication (AI copy-paste pattern). adapter/CLAUDE.md: 'Mapper functions in mapper.go, not inline in methods' — the table-specific dispatch should be a type parameter or a strategy argument, not duplicated function bodies.
deep_findings.recommendation: Introduce a generic or strategy-based helper (e.g., a `detailedLineUpsertStrategy` interface or a Go generics function parameterized on the Ent builder type) so both schema levels share a single upsert body. If generics are not practical, at minimum extract the shared loop logic into a helper that accepts a builder factory function.

deep_findings.title: OnCreditPurchasePaymentAuthorized and OnCreditPurchasePaymentSettled share near-identical bodies in chargeadapter
deep_findings.description: In `openmeter/ledger/chargeadapter/creditpurchase.go`, `OnCreditPurchasePaymentAuthorized` and `OnCreditPurchasePaymentSettled` execute the same sequence: (1) `charge.Validate()`, (2) zero-amount early return, (3) `charge.Intent.Settlement.GetCostBasis()`, (4) build `customerID`, (5) `chargeAnnotationsForCreditPurchaseCharge(charge)`, (6) `transactions.ResolveTransactions` with a different template type, (7) annotate each input with `transactions.WithAnnotations`, (8) `h.ledger.CommitGroup`. The documented pattern in `chargeadapter/CLAUDE.md` is 'Resolve-then-annotate-then-commit' applied identically in both methods; only the template differs. This is the same AI copy-paste anti-pattern identified in the pitfalls documentation.
deep_findings.pattern_reference: chargeadapter/CLAUDE.md: 'Resolve-then-annotate-then-commit — Every ledger write follows: transactions.ResolveTransactions → annotate each input with transactions.WithAnnotations → h.ledger.CommitGroup'. The pattern itself is correct; the violation is implementing it twice instead of once in a shared private helper.
deep_findings.recommendation: Extract a private `commitCreditPurchaseTemplate(ctx, charge, template)` helper that performs the resolve→annotate→commit sequence. `OnCreditPurchasePaymentAuthorized` and `OnCreditPurchasePaymentSettled` each call it with the appropriate template type.

deep_findings.title: createNewRealizationRun in run/create.go calls two adapter operations without an explicit transaction.Run wrapper
deep_findings.description: In `openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/service/run/create.go`, the private helper `createNewRealizationRun` calls `s.adapter.CreateRealizationRun` and then immediately calls `s.adapter.UpdateCharge` — two separate adapter mutations. These two writes are not wrapped in an explicit `transaction.Run` (or `entutils.TransactingRepo`) call within the run service itself. The outer `withLockedCharge` in the charges service does hold a transaction in ctx via `transaction.RunWithResult`, so the adapter calls land in the same Postgres transaction. However, the `run.Service` CLAUDE.md documents that the run service layer 'deliberately excludes state-machine decisions — it executes run operations' and should be self-contained with respect to atomicity. The flatfee service CLAUDE.md explicitly documents 'transaction.Run wraps all multi-step adapter calls'. The usagebased run service relying on an outer caller's transaction for atomicity is an implicit coupling that could break if `createNewRealizationRun` is ever called from a context where no transaction is already in ctx (e.g., in a test that drives run.Service directly).
deep_findings.pattern_reference: run/CLAUDE.md: 'Config-struct constructor with Validate() — Service intentionally has no state-machine logic'. AGENTS.md: 'In openmeter/billing/charges/.../adapter, always wrap Ent access with entutils.TransactingRepo even in shared helpers so the helper rebinds to the transaction already carried in ctx'. The adapter methods each use TransactingRepo individually, but the service layer should not rely on the outer caller to supply the transaction scope for a multi-step operation.
deep_findings.recommendation: Wrap the body of `createNewRealizationRun` (the two adapter calls) in a `transaction.RunWithResult` so the operation is self-contained and testable in isolation. The outer `withLockedCharge` transaction will be reused via ctx propagation (TransactingRepo will rebind to it), so there is no nesting risk.

deep_findings.title: lineengine.go newStateMachineForStandardLine fetches charge from adapter — billing engine layer reaching into charge persistence
deep_findings.description: In `openmeter/billing/charges/usagebased/service/lineengine.go`, `newStateMachineForStandardLine` directly calls `svc.GetByID` (the usagebased charges service) to fetch the charge entity when building a state machine for a standard billing line. The `LineEngine` is supposed to be a billing plugin that receives an already-resolved `billing.Line` and produces output — it is registered via `billing.Service.RegisterLineEngine` and invoked during invoice line processing. Fetching the charge inside the line engine couples the billing line processing layer to charge persistence reads. The CLAUDE.md for the run service notes that 'Service owns run mechanics but not state-machine decisions'; similarly, the line engine should receive the charge or have it resolved by the charges service before being passed in, rather than performing its own adapter reads inside the billing plugin callback.
deep_findings.pattern_reference: architecture.md billing_domain: 'billing.LineEngine interface declares GetLineEngineType() + OnCollectionCompleted + OnStandardInvoiceCreated + OnInvoiceIssued + OnPaymentAuthorized + OnPaymentSettled'. The engine is a callback plugin; adapter reads inside it violate the layering contract between the billing domain and the charges domain.
deep_findings.recommendation: The charge ID is already embedded in the billing line's metadata/reference. Consider passing the pre-fetched charge into the line engine trigger input, or having the charges service build a pre-loaded context before invoking the engine. This removes the adapter read from the billing plugin layer.
